context: Policy and market responses to the 2026 Iran war energy crisis (Operation Epic Fury, Feb 28 2026), which disrupted ~20% of global oil and 19% of LNG trade, sending Brent crude above $110/barrel.

policies.policy_name: RE Grid as Energy Security Shield
policies.quantitative_details: 60%+ electricity from renewables; wholesale prices €37–57/MWh vs €113 Germany, €141 Italy; gas sets price only 15% of time vs 89% in Italy
policies.context: Decades of RE investment now paying off as energy security shield; strong winds and heavy rains produced record hydro and wind output

policies.policy_name: €5B Comprehensive Response Plan to the Middle East Crisis
policies.quantitative_details: €5B total, 80 measures; VAT on energy cut 21%→10%; electricity bills cut 13%; petrol/diesel ~€0.30/L cheaper; 80% grid toll reduction for energy-intensive industry (~€200M savings); benefits 20M households, 3M businesses; in force until Jun 30, 2026
policies.context: PM Sánchez: 'Today we are more resilient thanks to renewables, and we must continue on this path'

policies.policy_name: Income Tax Deductions for Household RE Installations
policies.quantitative_details: Massive income tax deductions for rooftop solar, EV charging points, heat pumps; accelerated RE permitting; embedded in €5B crisis package
policies.context: Structural RE measure framed explicitly as investment in 'energy sovereignty', not climate policy

policies.policy_name: Clean Energy Investment Strategy and Citizens' Energy Package
policies.quantitative_details: €75B EIB clean energy financing over 3 years; €500M equity fund for grid operators; €500M pilot for 'energy efficiency as a service'; €200M InvestEU for SMRs
policies.context: Von der Leyen: crisis cost EU €3.5B extra in fossil imports in first 10 days alone

policies.quantitative_details: Current target: 42.5% RE by 2030; RE reached 25.2% of energy (2024), ~48% of electricity (2025); consultation open to Jun 12; legislative proposal by end 2026
policies.context: Consultation launched 20 days into crisis, directly linked to energy security imperative

policies.quantitative_details: Streamlining energy efficiency rules to support 90% emissions reduction by 2040
policies.context: Paired with RE framework as twin pillars of crisis response

policies.quantitative_details: €1.2T allocated for grid investment by 2040; 500+ GW new RE capacity; 8 cross-border 'Energy Highways'; 180 new transmission projects, 51 storage projects
policies.context: WindEurope CEO: 'Home-grown electricity is Europe's only future-proof energy strategy'

policies.quantitative_details: +12 GW onshore wind auction volumes through 2030 (~2,000 additional turbines); avoids 6.5 Mt CO₂; saves ~€1B/yr on gas imports; lowers wholesale prices by 0.6 ct/kWh; reduces gas consumption by 7 bcm by 2030
policies.context: Energy Minister Reiche: 'energy policy is now security policy'

policies.policy_name: Consumer Demand Surge for Distributed RE
policies.quantitative_details: E.ON solar inquiries doubled; 1KOMMA5° demand doubled vs prior month; Enpal demand at 2022 gas crisis levels; 40% jump in EV inquiries
policies.context: BSW: 'the energy price spike is opening many people's eyes'

policies.quantitative_details: AR8 brought forward to Jul 2026; up to 18 offshore wind farms eligible; prior AR7 secured record 14.7 GW across 201 projects; AR7 saves UK ~£4B/yr at current gas prices
policies.context: Miliband: 'There is no energy security while we are so dependent on fossil fuels'

policies.quantitative_details: All new homes in England to include solar panels + low-carbon heating (heat pumps) from 2028; plug-in solar panels to retail shops within months
policies.context: Miliband: 'The Iran War has once again shown our drive for clean power is essential for our energy security'

policies.quantitative_details: Up to £150M for zero-emission vessel development and deployment
policies.context: Decarbonizing shipping amid oil supply disruption

policies.policy_name: Climate Change Committee Net Zero Cost Analysis
policies.quantitative_details: Total cost of net zero by 2050 'no greater than the cost of a single fossil fuel price shock'; net-zero path would limit bill rise to 4% in crisis vs 59% without
policies.context: Published during crisis, powerfully reframing transition economics

policies.quantitative_details: Highest March nuclear generation since 2019; power exports nearly doubled YoY; Multiannual Energy Programme targets 380–420 TWh/yr nuclear by 2030–2035; 6 new EPR-2 reactors (10 GW)
policies.context: France leveraging existing low-carbon fleet to stabilize European prices

policies.policy_name: EV Market Surge as Energy Security Buffer
policies.quantitative_details: Used EV 'bonanza' on Finn.no marketplace; electricity costs €15.77/100 kWh vs €35.23 in Germany
policies.context: Long-term RE + EV investment insulates from price shocks

policies.quantitative_details: €100M industry support; gas sets electricity price 89% of time; power at €141/MWh
policies.context: Most exposed major EU economy; highlights cost of low RE penetration

policies.policy_name: Solar Boom as Energy Security Buffer
policies.quantitative_details: 51 GW solar panels imported by early 2026; solar provides ~20–25% of electricity; $12B oil/gas imports avoided since 2020; projected additional $6.3B savings in 2026; fossil imports fell 40% (2022–24)
policies.context: Consumer/market-led revolution enabled by zero-tariff Chinese panel imports since 2013

policies.quantitative_details: 4-day govt workweek; 50% WFH; 2-week school closure; 60% official vehicles grounded; 20% fuel price hike (largest in history); ministers forgo salaries; ~25–28 days fuel reserves
policies.context: Solar enabled WFH feasibility despite severe fuel shortage

policies.quantitative_details: Net metering shifted to net billing (export-import gap ~2:1); standard agreement cut from 7 to 5 years
policies.context: Controversial — critics say it undermines energy security by slowing the distributed solar growth that is Pakistan's strongest crisis buffer

policies.quantitative_details: Digital petroleum rationing introduced; Sindh province considering lockdown-type measures
policies.context: Extending limited fuel supplies with only 25–28 days reserves

policies.quantitative_details: 4-tier priority gas allocation; LPG refill minimum wait: 21→25 days; 48,000 KL additional kerosene authorized
policies.context: India imports ~60% of LPG from Gulf; 21 ships stalled in Strait of Hormuz

policies.quantitative_details: E-cooking now ~20% cheaper than LPG; Gurugram electricity consumption up 8M units in 9 days; AP deploying induction stoves in Anganwadi centres; ₹2/unit electricity subsidy for eateries switching to induction
policies.context: LPG shortage making 330M clean cooking connections fragile; electric cooking is fastest-growing crisis response

policies.policy_name: Existing RE Capacity as Resilience Buffer
policies.quantitative_details: 266 GW installed RE (~51% of total capacity); solar alone ~143 GW (50× since 2014); 500 GW target by 2030; ~35 GW faces curtailment risk from grid bottlenecks
policies.context: India more exposed than Pakistan despite larger RE base — heavier fossil import dependency

policies.quantitative_details: 100 GW RE target by 2030; 20% RE in generation by 2030; legislative reform removing local distance restrictions on solar; grid innovation plan within 6 months
policies.context: President Lee: crisis is 'a good opportunity to swiftly and extensively transition to renewable energy' — dramatic reversal from prior Yoon administration

policies.quantitative_details: Utilization raised from ~70% to 80%+; 2.8 GWe + 700 MW SMR planned by 2038; Saeul 3 (1,400 MW) expected late 2026
policies.context: Complementary to RE push; imports 94% of energy, 70% crude from Middle East

policies.quantitative_details: ₩26.2T (~$17.1B) total; ₩10.1T directly for oil price relief
policies.context: Stock market fell 18% in 4 trading days following Hormuz closure

policies.quantitative_details: 3 GW new transmission capacity beneath planned highway corridor to Yongin semiconductor cluster
policies.context: Critical for both semiconductor production reliability and RE integration

policies.quantitative_details: 14 of 54 reactors restarted post-Fukushima; nuclear at ~8% of electricity; aim to increase utilization further
policies.context: Imports 87% of energy; 95% of oil from Middle East

policies.quantitative_details: ~5.8–6 GW added in 2025, passing 100 GW cumulative; solar + wind at 11% of energy
policies.context: Experts calling for more investment; government restricting mega-solar but distributed solar growing

policies.quantitative_details: First country to declare energy emergency; 4-day govt workweek; AC ≥24°C; rooftop solar + storage promoted for diesel-dependent areas; 45 days reserves
policies.context: Imports 98% of oil from Middle East

policies.quantitative_details: Speeding up solar and geothermal; 2 SMRs by 2034 added to energy plan; 97% RE potential untapped
policies.context: 21–23 day fuel reserves; defence ministry introduced 1 remote work day/week

policies.policy_name: Solar Crisis Buffer and E10 Biofuel Acceleration
policies.quantitative_details: Solar saves 'hundreds of millions of dollars' in potential coal/gas imports; E10 gasoline rollout accelerated; zero fuel taxes until Apr 15, 2026
policies.context: Trade ministry urged WFH (Mar 10); airlines cutting flights 10–50%

policies.policy_name: EV Incentives and Government Fleet Electrification
policies.quantitative_details: Slashed EV service fees; ordered govt agencies not to buy new gas vehicles; 3-day school week; 4-day workweek
policies.context: Landlocked, impoverished; hundreds of filling stations closed

policies.policy_name: Fuel Efficiency Mandates and EV Promotion
policies.quantitative_details: Strict fuel efficiency for state bodies; fuel taxes cut; fuel prices rose 68%; ~33% of 6,300 petrol stations shut; electric mobility promoted
policies.context: Energy Minister advocating ASEAN-wide power grid

policies.quantitative_details: Excise halved from 52.6→26.3 c/L for 3 months; $2.55B cost; saves ~A$19/tank; heavy vehicle charge zeroed
policies.context: Imports >90% refined oil; lowest IEA member oil stocks (~36 days)

policies.policy_name: RE and Electrification as Crisis Buffer (Advocacy)
policies.quantitative_details: 45% of main grid electricity from RE/storage; 4M+ households with solar; 454,000 EVs on road (13% new sales); FBT EV exemption costs $9.7B over 2026–30
policies.context: IEEFA: 'the perfect storm to boost energy security'; Climate Council: transition is the cure, not the cause

policies.policy_name: EV Import Mandate and Hydropower Buffer
policies.quantitative_details: Banned gasoline/diesel car imports (2024); EVs grew 1%→6% of market; 95% renewable electricity (Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam)
policies.context: Economists argue Ethiopia could 'avoid the worst of a global recession' via electric mobility

policies.quantitative_details: Mandatory 9pm shop/restaurant/mall closure weekdays (10pm weekends); street lights dimmed; billboards off; partial remote work; govt vehicle fuel cut ~30%; monthly energy bill tripled to $1.65B; fuel prices raised up to 30%
policies.context: Ministers insist 'investment in renewable energy will continue to expand'

policies.quantitative_details: Increasing ethanol content in petrol to stretch fuel supplies
policies.context: Immediate fossil fuel demand reduction via domestic biofuel substitution

policies.policy_name: Solar Rooftop Mandate for New Buildings
policies.quantitative_details: All new residential buildings must install solar (min 5 kW); non-residential >250 sqm roof; ~10,000 new homes/year; 15% annual ROI; 30% RE target by 2030 (from ~15%); needs 18–23 GW solar + 5.5 GW/33 GWh storage
policies.context: Policy enacted before crisis but directly relevant to energy security imperative

policies.quantitative_details: 34 solar parks on grid, ~560 MW peak; ~38% of daytime generation from solar; 24% RE target by 2030; full transition needs $8–10B over decade
policies.context: Separate US oil blockade since Jan 2026; island-wide blackout Mar 16; analysts warn 'too little, too late' without battery storage

policies.quantitative_details: Encouraging walking/non-fossil transport; decentralizing activities to local level; drastically cutting consumption
policies.context: Near-total fossil fuel cutoff from combined Iran crisis and US blockade

policies.quantitative_details: 5 offshore wind projects halted; TotalEnergies paid ~$1B to abandon 2 wind leases redirected to oil/gas; IRA wind/solar credits truncated (construction by end 2026, completion by end 2027)
policies.context: Rhodium: 53–59% reduction in new clean power capacity over next decade

policies.quantitative_details: Trump shared pro-rooftop-solar video; Interior reviewing ~20 stuck solar projects; residential 30% ITC eliminated by OBBBA (effective Jan 1, 2026)
policies.context: Paradox: crisis strengthens RE case while federal incentives removed

policies.policy_name: Consumer EV Interest Surge (No Policy Support)
policies.quantitative_details: Gas up 27% to $4.29/gal ($6.81 SF); CarEdge EV searches up 20%; Edmunds: electrified vehicle shopping rose 20.7%→22.4%; Chevy EV searches up 30%+
policies.context: Consumer interest surging despite federal EV subsidy repeal

policies.policy_name: High EV Adoption as Energy Security Buffer
policies.quantitative_details: 70%+ of new car sales are electric
policies.context: High EV adoption insulates from oil price spikes; long-term policy investment paying off

policies.quantitative_details: Vehicle refueling limits; fuel rationing; early Ramadan holidays; universities closed; malls/restaurants closed at 9pm; temporary blackouts
policies.context: Imports 95% of energy; half electricity from gas

policies.policy_name: Fuel Rationing and Public Holiday Mandate
policies.quantitative_details: Every Wednesday declared public holiday; 15 L/week for motorists; 200 L for public transport; QR-based National Fuel Pass
policies.context: President: 'We must prepare for the worst'

policies.quantitative_details: Alternating driving days by license plate number; QR codes to prevent multiple refills
policies.context: Extreme fuel shortage

policies.quantitative_details: 400M barrels released (>2× previous record); one-third of total government stockpiles
policies.context: Largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market'

policies.policy_name: RE Acceleration Statements and 2026 Crisis Policy Tracker Launch
policies.quantitative_details: Renewables = 85% of all new global power capacity in 2025; 90%+ of new RE projects cheaper than fossil alternatives; launched 2026 Energy Crisis Policy Response Tracker
policies.context: Birol: 'I expect one of the responses will be acceleration of renewables — homegrown domestic energy source'

policies.quantitative_details: WFH; cut highway speed by 10 km/h; switch to electric cooking; carpooling; divert LPG to essential uses; industrial efficiency
policies.context: Supply-side measures alone cannot fully offset the scale of the disruption'

policies.policy_name: Executive Secretary Stiell Statement on Fossil Dependency
policies.quantitative_details: Called fossil dependence 'ripping away national security and sovereignty'; speech at Green Growth Summit
policies.context: Sunlight doesn't depend on narrow and vulnerable shipping straits. Wind blows without massive taxpayer-funded naval escorts'

policies.policy_name: Energy Transition Finance and Crisis Analysis Publications
policies.quantitative_details: Global energy transition investment reached $2.4T in 2024 (20% increase); RE investment at $807B; grid investment needs $671B/yr through 2030; COP29 target: 1,500 GW storage by 2030
policies.context: Ongoing analytical support for transition amid crisis

policies.policy_name: Special Foreign Ministers Meeting on Energy Security
policies.quantitative_details: Joint statement on energy security/resilience; referenced ASEAN Renewable Energy Long-Term Roadmap; member states' efficiency measures projected to reduce demand by 32.7% by 2050
policies.context: Redoubling efforts to switch from fossil fuels to renewables'

policies.policy_name: Emergency Joint Finance, Energy and Central Bank Meeting
policies.quantitative_details: First combined finance/energy/central bank format in G7 history since 1975; Energy Commissioner called for 'reducing fossil imports, boosting RE production, establishing energy union'
policies.context: Fourth ministerial-level G7 meeting since war began; produced 'few actionable outcomes'

policies.quantitative_details: Called crisis 'Asia's Ukraine moment'; scaling up EVs could save importers >$600B/yr in oil imports; Pakistan solar saved $12B+ since 2020
policies.context: Urgency of Asia's renewables pivot for macroeconomic stability'

policies.quantitative_details: TEST
policies.context: TEST
